claim.content: The noninteracting Bistritzer-MacDonald continuum model of $1.05^{\circ}$ twisted bilayer graphene, with Dirac velocity $v_{F}=10^{6}~\mathrm{m/s}$ in both graphene layers and intra- and inter-sublattice tunneling parameters $w_{0}=77~\mathrm{meV}$ and $w_{1}=110~\mathrm{meV}$, yields band dispersions and Bloch wave functions that reproduce the qualitative tunneling singularity phenomenology of interest, including inverse-root onsets, coherence peaks, and cone-shaped low-bias features, when used in momentum-conserving tunneling simulations. This approximation assumes that interaction-driven renormalizations in actual magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene do not qualitatively alter the geometric origin of the tangency-induced singularities.
